title: Tristate error on inout port connections
body:
Issue:

Trying to concatenate scalar inout ports to connect to a submodule triggers a REPLICATE error. The same pattern when done with replication of bits from a vectors does not trigger an error. Either there is a missing error on the vector or too many errors on the scalar VDD replication? Not using this for simulation, just for implementation, but want to maintain a single golden source.  I don't think this is a Verilog restriction.

Error-UNSUPPORTED: test.v:7:24: Unsupported LHS tristate construct: REPLICATE

Version: Verilator 5.030 2024-10-27 rev v5.030
[UPDATE]: Same behavior on Verilator 5.043 devel rev v5.042-106-g9155e2529

Icarus does not trigger the error.

Test case:

```verilog
module top(inout       VSS,
           inout       VDD,
           inout [1:0] GPIO);

   sub sub(.pads  ({GPIO[0],GPIO[0]}),
           .VDD   ({VDD,VDD}),
           .VSS   ({VSS,VSS}));

endmodule


module sub(inout [1:0] pads,
           inout [1:0] VDD,
           inout [1:0] VSS
           );
endmodule


```
